_source: Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE) India — SME Cluster Energy Benchmarks
_references: BEE-SIDBI WB-GEF Case Study: Pune Forging Cluster, M/s P5 unit (532 tpa, 63 toe baseline)
BEE-SME Programme: Ludhiana Forging Cluster — energy savings 20-40% post induction heater
BEE Impact Report 2021-22, Chapter 3: MSME Sector benchmarks
UNIDO Foundry Best Practice Guide — SEC ranges for hot forging
_units: kWh per tonne of finished product
_notes: Pune case study unit baseline: ~1375 kWh/tonne (FO-fired, <8% furnace efficiency). Post-induction retrofit: ~700 kWh/tonne. Ludhiana cluster verified 20-40% savings over baseline.

mild_steel.notes: Hot forging with induction heating. Typical verified against Pune cluster BEE-SIDBI case study. Max = FO-fired box furnace <8% efficiency (Pune baseline 1375 kWh/tonne).
mild_steel.source_datapoint: Pune M/s P5: 63 toe / 532 tpa = 1375 kWh/tonne baseline; post-induction ~700 kWh/tonne

alloy_steel.notes: Higher SEC due to hardness and controlled heating cycles. Alloy grades need tighter temperature windows.

stainless_steel.notes: Requires controlled atmosphere and precise temperature 1100-1250 deg C range.

aluminium.notes: Forging at 380-500 deg C. Significantly lower SEC than steel.

aluminium_alloy.notes: Alloys (6061, 7075) — slightly higher SEC than pure aluminium due to tighter temperature tolerance.

emission_factors._source: IPCC AR5 / World Steel Association / International Aluminium Institute

grid_emission_factor._source: CEA CO2 Baseline Database for Indian Power Sector, Version 18 (2023-24)
